conversation.text_en: Hi, I'm an AI mental health assistant — everything we discuss here is completely confidential, so please feel comfortable. How was your week leading up to our appointment today?

Oh wow, okay, an AI — I wasn't sure what to expect honestly, but knowing it stays between us does help. The drive was a bit stressful actually, I got stuck in traffic on the highway and arrived later than I wanted.
Sorry to hear that. Can you tell me a little bit about your daily routine? What's been going on at work as a real estate agent?
Well, it's been really tough, to be honest. I've been struggling to get out of bed in the morning and getting to the office on time is a challenge too. Sometimes I just want to go back home.
That sounds like it's affecting your work. How have you been handling client interactions and meetings?

Honestly, I feel like I'm just going through the motions. I don't have the same energy or enthusiasm that I used to. And sometimes I find myself getting really anxious about making mistakes or not being able to meet their expectations.
I understand how frustrating that must be. Can you tell me more about your social life and relationships? Do you feel like you're withdrawing from friends and family?
Yeah, definitely. I used to love spending time with my friends, but lately, I just don't have the energy or interest in doing anything fun. And my partner has been noticing a change too.
How do you think your partner is handling this change? Have you talked to them about how you're feeling?
Honestly, we haven't really talked about it much. I feel like they're getting frustrated with me because I'm not the same person anymore. And I don't know what to say or how to make things better.
It sounds like there's a lot of uncertainty and stress in your relationships right now. Have you noticed any changes in your sleep patterns?
Yeah, actually. I've been having trouble falling asleep and staying asleep too. Sometimes I wake up multiple times during the night and can't get back to sleep.
That sounds really challenging. Can you tell me more about what's going on when you're trying to fall asleep? Are you feeling stressed or anxious?

Yeah, definitely. My mind just keeps racing with all these thoughts and worries. And sometimes I'll have trouble falling asleep because I'm thinking about work or things that need to get done the next day.
It sounds like your brain is really active at night. Have you noticed any changes in your appetite or eating habits?
Yeah, actually. I've been having a hard time eating lately. Sometimes I'll force myself to eat just so I don't starve, but other times I'll forget about food altogether.
That can be really challenging. Can you tell me more about what's going on when you're eating? Do you feel like your appetite is affected by stress or anxiety?
Yeah, definitely. When I'm feeling stressed or anxious, my appetite just disappears. And sometimes I'll eat because I'm bored or emotional.
That makes sense. Have you been doing anything to take care of yourself and manage your stress levels? Do you have any hobbies or activities that bring you joy?

Honestly, no. I used to love hiking and spending time outdoors, but lately, it feels like too much effort. And my partner has been trying to get me to do things with them, but I just don't have the energy.
It sounds like there's a lot of withdrawal going on right now. Can you tell me more about what's happening when you're trying to focus or concentrate?

Yeah, actually. Sometimes I'll be trying to read something or work on a project and my mind will just wander off. I'll get distracted by the smallest thing and then forget what I was doing in the first place.
That sounds really frustrating. Have you noticed any physical sensations or body feelings that might be related to your stress levels?
Yeah, actually. Sometimes I'll get these weird headaches or my shoulders will ache from tension. And sometimes I'll feel like I'm just exhausted all the time.
That can be really uncomfortable. Can you tell me more about what's been going on in your personal life? Have there been any recent stressful events or changes that might be contributing to this?
Yeah, actually. My mom was diagnosed with cancer recently and it's been really tough for me. I feel like I'm just trying to keep everything together while taking care of her.
I'm so sorry to hear that. It sounds like you're carrying a lot of responsibility right now. Do you have any support systems in place, such as friends or family members who can help with caregiving?
Yeah, actually. My partner has been really helpful and my siblings are trying their best too.
That's great to hear. What are your goals for our work together? What do you hope to achieve from this therapy process?

Honestly, I just want to feel like myself again. I want to be able to enjoy things without feeling drained or anxious all the time. And I want to be able to take care of my mom and support her through this tough time.
Those are really important goals. Before we wrap up today, is there anything that's been on your mind that you haven't had a chance to share with me yet?
Actually, yeah. I've just been feeling really guilty about not being able to do everything like I used to. And sometimes I feel like I'm letting my mom down because I can't be the strong one for her.

It sounds like there's a lot of self-criticism going on right now. Remember that we're here to work together and support you in getting your life back on track. What do you think is the most important thing we should focus on moving forward?
I think we should focus on finding ways for me to manage my stress and anxiety, so I can start enjoying things again. And maybe working on setting some realistic boundaries with work and family.
Those are great goals. Remember, our next session is in two weeks. Please make sure to schedule it beforehand, okay?
Yeah, got it. Thank you for today, it really helped me feel like I'm not alone in this.
